mission.title: Install and Configure Kind (Kubernetes in Docker)
mission.description: Kind (Kubernetes in Docker) is a tool for running local Kubernetes clusters using Docker container nodes. It is primarily designed for testing Kubernetes itself but can also be used for local development or CI. Key trade-offs include the requirement for Docker and the fact that it is not suitable for production workloads.

steps.title: Install Kind
steps.description:
This step installs Kind using the precompiled binary for your platform.

```bash

[ $(uname -m) = x86_64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64

[ $(uname -m) = aarch64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-arm64

chmod +x ./kind

sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> For AMD64 / x86_64 on Linux
> For ARM64 on Linux
> Make the binary executable
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a local Kubernetes cluster using Kind.

```bash

kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0

```

> Creates a cluster with Kubernetes version 1.31.0

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step sets up the kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(kind get kubeconfig-path --name=

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable to use the Kind cluster

steps.title: Verify the Cluster
steps.description:
This step verifies that the cluster is running and nodes are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the status of the nodes in the cluster

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step deletes the Kind cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will remove all workloads and data in the cluster.

```bash

kind delete cluster

```

> Removes the Kind cluster

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kind
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step upgrades Kind to the latest version.

```bash

curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64

chmod +x ./kind

sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> Download the latest Kind binary
> Make the binary executable
> Replace the old Kind binary with the new one

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kind: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kind binary is not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure that /usr/local/bin is in your PATH or move the Kind binary to a directory that is in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: failed to start nodes'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker is not running or there are insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Start Docker and ensure you have enough CPU and memory allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet is unhealthy'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes node is not starting properly.

**Fix:**
Check Docker logs for the Kind node container and ensure Docker has enough resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to get kubeconfig'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kind cluster is not created or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Recreate the Kind cluster using 'kind create cluster'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.31.0, improved error messages during cluster creation.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing the nodes in the Ready state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure Docker is installed and running on your machine.
